content:
Search for documentation on the oh-my-opencode configuration schema. Specifically find:
1. The official configuration schema URL or documentation
2. All available agents that can be configured
3. Tmux configuration settings and available options
4. Skills configuration format and sources
5. Best practices and examples
6. Required vs optional fields
<!-- OMO_INTERNAL_INITIATOR -->

[![@FurryWolfX](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/12652119?u=939885c9ef473f9b9a890b2cf0a9d7e0e02e6b0b&v=4&size=80)](https://github.com/FurryWolfX)

## Description

[![@FurryWolfX](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/12652119?u=939885c9ef473f9b9a890b2cf0a9d7e0e02e6b0b&v=4&size=48)](https://github.com/FurryWolfX)

[FurryWolfX](https://github.com/FurryWolfX)

opened [on Feb 12, 2026](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/1781#issue-3929502264)

Issue body actions

### Prerequisites

-    I will write this issue in English (see our [Language Policy](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/blob/dev/CONTRIBUTING.md#language-policy))
-    I have searched existing issues to avoid duplicates
-    I am using the latest version of oh-my-opencode
-    I have read the [documentation](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode#readme) or asked an AI coding agent with this project's GitHub URL loaded and couldn't find the answer

### Bug Description

After switching from `background_task` to `task` tool, the tmux panel management for parallel subagent execution is broken. When running multiple tasks in parallel , only the last subagent's panel is displayed, with previous panels being covered/overwritten.

### Steps to Reproduce

1.  Trigger multiple parallel tasks
2.  Note that only the last task's panel is visible; previous panels are missing

### Expected Behavior

All subagent panels should be visible simultaneously in separate tmux panels/windows, allowing monitoring of multiple parallel tasks at the same time.

### Actual Behavior

Only the last started subagent's panel is shown. Earlier panels disappear or get overlapped, making it impossible to monitor multiple tasks concurrently.

### Doctor Output

#\# Environment
- OS: WSL2 (Ubuntu 24.04)
- Version: OpenCode 1.1.59, OMO 3.5.2, tmux 3.4
- Terminal: Windows Terminal

### Error Logs

### Configuration

{
  "tmux": {
    "enabled": true,
    "layout": "main-vertical",
    "main\_pane\_size": 60,
    "main\_pane\_min\_width": 120,
    "agent\_pane\_min\_width": 40
  }
}

### Additional Context

*No response*

### Operating System

Linux

### OpenCode Version

1.1.59

[![@aberey](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19147325?u=180de02a5b6593cfe0595ac3533086afb22a47e1&v=4&size=80)](https://github.com/aberey)

## Description

[![@aberey](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19147325?u=180de02a5b6593cfe0595ac3533086afb22a47e1&v=4&size=48)](https://github.com/aberey)

[aberey](https://github.com/aberey)

opened [on Mar 2, 2026](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/2260#issue-4013936059)

Issue body actions

### Prerequisites

-    I will write this issue in English (see our [Language Policy](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/blob/dev/CONTRIBUTING.md#language-policy))
-    I have searched existing issues to avoid duplicates
-    I am using the latest version of oh-my-opencode
-    I have read the [documentation](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode#readme) or asked an AI coding agent with this project's GitHub URL loaded and couldn't find the answer

### Bug Description

Even with `tmux.enabled=true`, subagents won't spawn in separate tmux panes.  
Based on code analysis, this is due to the health check at [https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/blob/dev/src/shared/tmux/tmux-utils/server-health.ts#L13](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/blob/dev/src/shared/tmux/tmux-utils/server-health.ts#L13) using `/health` as the endpoint path to check server health (which returns a http 403 due to the endpoint not existing, therefore skipping tmux integration), while the actual endpoint in opencode is `/global/health` (see [https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/global.ts#L18-L21](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/global.ts#L18-L21)).

### Steps to Reproduce

1.  Configure oh-my-openocde.json with `{ "tmux": { "enabled":true } }`
2.  start opencode with `opencode -p 4096`
3.  submit a query that uses subagents

### Expected Behavior

agents should spawn in separate tmux pane splits

### Actual Behavior

agents run in same window

### Doctor Output

Opencode version: 1.2.15
oh-my-opencode version: 3.9.0

### Error Logs

### Configuration

### Additional Context

*No response*

### Operating System

macOS

### OpenCode Version

1.2.15

Reactions are currently unavailable

## Description

[![@cheuklcl](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/251124781?u=f689860edff6de46c690ec0c083fb4a08367ad88&v=4&size=48)](https://github.com/cheuklcl)

[cheuklcl](https://github.com/cheuklcl)

opened [on Mar 20, 2026](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent/issues/2729#issue-4109202384)

Issue body actions

### Prerequisites

-    I will write this issue in English (see our [Language Policy](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode/blob/dev/CONTRIBUTING.md#language-policy))
-    I have searched existing issues to avoid duplicates
-    I am using the latest version of oh-my-opencode
-    I have read the [documentation](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode#readme) or asked an AI coding agent with this project's GitHub URL loaded and couldn't find the answer

### Bug Description

oh-my-opencode tmux subagent panes work for me when running OpenCode locally inside tmux, but fail in opencode serve + opencode attach mode.  
After tracing logs, the failure is not caused by tmux gating anymore. The tmux manager reaches spawnTmuxPane(), but the serverUrl passed into the spawn path is [http://127.0.0.1:0/](http://127.0.0.1:0/), so health checks fail and no tmux pane is created.

### Steps to Reproduce

1.  Start OpenCode backend inside tmux:  
    export OPENCODE\_SERVER\_PASSWORD='\*\*\*'  
    export OPENCODE\_SERVER\_USERNAME='opencode'  
    opencode serve --hostname 127.0.0.1 --port 54220
    
2.  In another pane/window in the same tmux session:  
    export OPENCODE\_SERVER\_PASSWORD='***'  
    export OPENCODE\_SERVER\_USERNAME='opencode'  
    tmux set-environment -g OPENCODE\_SERVER\_PASSWORD '***'  
    tmux set-environment -g OPENCODE\_SERVER\_USERNAME 'opencode'  
    opencode attach [http://127.0.0.1:54220](http://127.0.0.1:54220)
    
3.  Inside the attached OpenCode session, launch a background subagent, for example:
    
    -   task(... run\_in\_background=true ...)
    -   or any prompt that reliably creates background subagents
4.  Check tmux panes:  
    tmux list-panes -F '#{pane\_id} #{pane\_title}'
    

### Expected Behavior

A new tmux pane should be created for the background subagent and attach to the subagent session.

### Actual Behavior

No new tmux pane is created.

### Doctor Output

\- macOS
- tmux 3.6a
- OpenCode backend started with opencode serve
- OpenCode client connected with opencode attach
- oh-my-opencode user config:
{
  "tmux": {
    "enabled": true,
    "layout": "main-vertical"
  }
}

### Error Logs

### Configuration

### Additional Context

Evidence  
Relevant log lines from /tmp/oh-my-opencode.log / temp log path:  
\[spawnTmuxPane\] called {"sessionId":"ses\_...","description":"Map repo patterns (@explore subagent)","serverUrl":"[http://127.0.0.1:0/","configEnabled":true,"targetPaneId":"%17","splitDirection":"-h"}](http://127.0.0.1:0/%22,%22configEnabled%22:true,%22targetPaneId%22:%22%17%22,%22splitDirection%22:%22-h%22%7D)  
\[spawnTmuxPane\] SKIP: server not running {"serverUrl":"[http://127.0.0.1:0/"}](http://127.0.0.1:0/%22%7D)  
\[tmux-session-manager\] deferred session attach failed {"sessionId":"ses\_...","results":\[{"type":"spawn","success":false}\]}  
I also confirmed that at this stage the runtime does see tmux correctly:  
TMUX=/private/tmp/tmux-501/default,...  
TMUX\_PANE=%17  
So the failure is no longer due to isInsideTmux=false. It is specifically due to serverUrl being [http://127.0.0.1:0/](http://127.0.0.1:0/).  
Suspected root cause  
TmuxSessionManager appears to trust ctx.serverUrl directly:  
const defaultPort = process.env.OPENCODE\_PORT ?? "4096"  
this.serverUrl = ctx.serverUrl?.toString() ?? `http://localhost:${defaultPort}`  
In my serve + attach setup, ctx.serverUrl seems to resolve to [http://127.0.0.1:0/](http://127.0.0.1:0/).  
That value then flows through:

-   TmuxSessionManager
-   handleSessionCreated()
-   executeActions()
-   spawnTmuxPane()  
    and fails health check before any pane can be split.  
    Notes  
    This does not reproduce for me when running OpenCode locally inside tmux without the serve + attach flow. In that case, tmux panes can be created successfully.  
    It looks like serve + attach mode may expose an invalid ctx.serverUrl to the plugin, or the plugin may need a fallback when ctx.serverUrl.port === "0".  
    Possible fix directions
-   Treat ctx.serverUrl with port 0 as invalid and fall back to a real attach/base URL
-   Fall back to OPENCODE\_PORT or another reliable host-provided value
-   Validate ctx.serverUrl before using it in tmux pane spawning

### Operating System

macOS

### OpenCode Version

1.2.27

Reactions are currently unavailable
